lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Greetings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Well, friend, I'm Arls Lutwidge of the Moonlight Broadsword! Renowned all over Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't say I've heard of you before.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: You haven't... heard of... That's madness!

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Surely you've heard of my feats, but perhaps not the name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: And yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An adventurer mostly, but I've slain some dragons and I've conquered some nightmares.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Very good, young one! Very good indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... where's this famous sword of yours?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Gone. I gave it to a fledgling when I retired and the damned fool went and died fighting some great monster in a far off land.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Say... if you find it, could you bring it to me? I won't pay you, as holding such a magnificent weapon should be payment enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you need to work on your bartering skills.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Perhaps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What grand feats are you known for?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: I peaked rather early and most know me for my first act of heroism when I defeated the Moonfly. It may sound cute, but it killed many of the smallfolk and their livestock, for it was a vile creature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where'd it come from?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: It was I that discovered the creature. I pulled it from the Lum during a fishing trip of mine.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: As young and foolish as I was, I dropped it down a dried up well and went for help. However, upon my return, the creature was gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then what happened?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Many years passed until suddenly there were sightings of a winged demon around the River Lum. Some claimed it was an overly large heron and that it was nothing to be worried about.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: As time went on, folk started to go missing and I knew I had to return home and face this demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You left?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: I had wars to fight, glory to be won and all that rubbish. Upon my return I spoke to an old crone who lived in the swamps and she gave me some advice.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: She told me to spike my armour and described how to lure the creature toward me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you defeated it? The Moonfly?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Some bard wrote a poem of the events. The telling of the battle is a little embellished and rife with errors. The poem can be found in this library somewhere, providing it has been returned.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: And did I defeat the creature? I wouldn't be here if I hadn't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be sure to keep an eye out.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Please do! And come to me if you have any burning questions about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: I've grown old, friend. Heroes need to know when to throw in the towel, so they say. I've always enjoyed a good book, so it seemed fitting to stay here as librarian for the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... the books are very interesting. I thought there would be more though...

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Well, that's why I'm here! So many of you adventurers help yourself to any old book and never return the damn things!

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: If I could have it my way, you wouldn't be allowed to wander off with them, but I'm not the guildmaster, as Alec likes to remind me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certainly sounds like you have a problem.

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Well fear not. I'm working on it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: I'll see you around.

sections.title: Using Lutwidge and the moonfly on Lutwidge

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Yes! Fantastic! Did you enjoy it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah it was alright. Some of these words are a bit odd though. Did you really cover your sword in sheep blood?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Ah, a slight misunderstanding... I soaked it in sap from a yew tree, but I guess blood is a bit more dramatic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: The crone, long dead now, I suppose... She claimed to be from the same world as the creature and knew of the ritual to summon it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The poem does mention sheep though, at the end?

lines.speaker: Lutwidge
lines.text: Perhaps some locals claimed to have lost some sheep, but my guess is that the bard was searching for a rhyme. Either way, I doubt it's a detail worth fretting over.
